name: The Next Curve - Meetup
description:
The Next Curve is a community for people who believe that life can become richer, healthier, and more meaningful with age.
We are on a mission to reinvent ageing for people and advocate for a healthier planet through awareness around regenerative agriculture and conscious living.
At The Next Curve, we explore the intersection of physical health, emotional well-being, mental resilience, purpose, relationships, nutrition, longevity, and environmental responsibility. Through conversations, walks, workshops, expert talks, community gatherings, and shared experiences, we aim to create a space where people support one another in living better while contributing to a better world.
This is not a community defined by age.
It is a community defined by mindset.
Whether you are 25 or 75, if you care about your health, your future, and the future of the planet, you belong here.
Our Belief
Because Healthy People Need a Healthy Planet.
The Next Curve
Growing Older. Living Better. Becoming More. 🌿

name: Rhyme & Rasam: Poetry That’s Easy to Love - Poetry Discussion
description:
Come, enjoy an evening of poetry with us as the monsoon settles in. This is a guided reading with carefully chosen poems. We won't torture the poems through over-analysis. We will examine them lightly and read them in context.

Theme: Monsoon Poems 
You don't even have to love poetry to join. Come for some literary comfort and some surprises. Depth of flavour assured!
Tea & coffee included
Hosted by writer and poet Suchi Govindarajan

name: Storywalk Through Indiranagar - Story Walk by Vikram Sridhar
description: On a Saturday morning, walk through the lanes of INDIRA NAGAR with Vikram Sridhar, discovering the lanes through the folklore, heritage, and Ecology of the area. The walk will begin and end at ATTA GALATTA. The tales will revolve around historical, mythical, and personal narratives woven as we discover the area through the walk.

name: Food In Literature - Book Club
description:
Food is more than sustenance; it is a map of our memories and a bridge to different cultures. This  month, we continue our dialogue on the narratives that connect what we read to what we eat as we follow the story of a Japanese novelist and her interpreter as they eat their way through colonial Taiwan.
Book of the month: Taiwan Travelogue written Yáng Shuāng-zǐ  translated by Lin King
When: Saturday, 27th June, 2026 at 11 a.m.
Where: Atta Galatta Bookstore, Indira Nagar
Ages: 18+
Hosted by: Prachi Grover
Registration is mandatory as we have limited spots (link in bio)
The session is free & copies of the book are available at the store. 
Prachi Grover is a culinary consultant and educator and is the founder of “From Words to Wok”; a 
community for people who to love to read and eat

name: Crochet Rose Workshop - Crochet Workshop
description:
Always wanted to learn crochet?
Join Anaahat for a beginner-friendly workshop and create your own handmade crochet rose to take home.
What you will learn: 

Basic crochet techniques
Reading simple crochet patterns
Creating a crochet rose step-by-step
Finishing and assembly techniques

Meet your instructor 
Anaahat is a young crochet artist and founder of Anaahat’s Keepsake Studio. She creates handmade crochet keepsakes and enjoys introducing beginners to the craft.

name: Atta Galatta presents “Let Poetry Be” - Poetry Open Mic
description:
This monthly gathering will see poetry coming to life, through reading, music, art and any other forms of artistic expression, at Atta Galatta. Listeners and performers are all welcome!
 
Date: 27th June
Time: 4pm 
Venue: Atta Galatta, Indiranagar
 
Event specifics & submission guidelines –
 
1. Theme - Weather 
 
2. Works can be original or of other poets, performed live
3. All languages welcome
4. Participants can read to music (acoustic instrument/s), dramatise, use props & costumes
5. Each participant will enjoy 5 minutes of reading time
6. Kindly submit your poem and name to letpoetrybe@gmail.com on or before 25th June midnight. WE WILL NOT ACCEPT ATTACHMENTS OR LINKS. SUBJECT OF YOUR MAIL MUST READ AS 'Entry for-June LPB meet-up'
7. You may also send entries that are not related to the theme
8. The event will begin at 4pm sharp and will be held at Atta Galatta. Participants to report not later than 3:45 pm to hear about performance order and meet the host
9. For any further clarifications, write to us at letpoetrybe@gmail.com
 
You are now welcome to flood our inbox!

name: Paper and Pride- A DIY Pocket Magazine Workshop - Art Workshop
description:
Join us for a messy, creative evening of making a handmade mini-magazine from scratch. We are filling pages with everything that makes us who we are: identity, pride, culture, and hard-learned lessons.You design page by page. We bind them together. Everyone takes a copy of the finished mini magazine home. Real. Unfiltered. 
Made by actual humans. We're making one together - collaborative pages around identity, pride (literal, cultural, professional, queer, all of it), what you stand for, things you're learning the hard way. It's messy. It's supposed to be. By the end, we have a real handmade zine that everyone contributed to. You take it home. You flip through it later and remember this night. 
This is open to LGBT+ community and allies. Everyone. Because pride isn't one thing.

name: Neeraj Pandey's Goodboy - Dramatised storytelling
description:
A 70 mins dramatised storytelling show written and performed by the lyricist, screenwriter and Author Neeraj Pandey.

Synopsis: Two cousins who were once inseparable haven’t spoken in ten years. A phone call about one’s hospitalisation forces the other to
make the visit. Would they be able to look beyond what broke between them?

name: Tinsy Doo Tells A Tale - Storytelling & Book Signing
description:
Join author Aarushi Jain for a delightful storytelling session featuring a story from her children’s book - Tiny Tales with Tinsy Doo.
An engaging experience filled with imagination, creativity, and fun for children and parents, followed by an exclusive book signing session.
Age: 4-8 yrs

name: Handmade Book Binding Workshop - Book Binding Workshop
description:
In this hands-on workshop, participants will be introduced to the fundamentals of bookbinding. They will learn the basics of the craft, including key terminology, tools, and materials commonly used in traditional bookbinding practices.
 
The session will focus on creating a handmade book using the Coptic stitch binding technique, a historical and visually striking method known for its exposed spine and ability to lay completely flat when open. Each participant will complete their own book by the end of the session, gaining practical experience in folding, punching, stitching, and assembling the components.
 
This workshop is ideal for beginners as well as creative enthusiasts interested in crafts, journaling, or book arts.

name: Funny Games - Film Screening
description: A family's vacation at a secluded lakeside home takes an unsettling turn following the arrival of two young strangers. What begins as a seemingly ordinary encounter gradually erodes the family's sense of security, creating an atmosphere of mounting tension. Through this premise, the film explores themes of control, spectatorship, and the audience's relationship with cinematic violence. The screening will be followed by a moderated discussion examining the film through the lens of meta-cinema.

name: Read A Kitaab - Book Club
description:
Six months. Countless pages. Unforgettable characters. Books abandoned, books devoured, and maybe a few that changed the way you see the world.
Join us for the June edition of the Read A Kitaab Bangalore Meet-up as we look back on our reading journey through the first half of 2026. Share your best reads, biggest disappointments, unexpected favourites, reading goals, and everything in between.
Whether you’ve read 2 books or 200, there’s a seat for your story. ❤️
📅 Sunday, 21st June 2026
⏰ 5 AM – 7 PM
📍 Atta Galatta, Indira Nagar
🎯 Theme: Halfway Through 2026 – A Conversation About Your Reading Journey
Entry is free. Registration is mandatory.
Come for the books. Stay for the conversations. Leave with a longer TBR

name: Bangalore Finance Club Presents: The Creator Economy - Content, Capital, and The Economics of Being an Influencer
description:
Is content creation just a hobby, or is it the ultimate modern asset class?
For the Bangalore Finance Club’s 3rd event, we are diving deep into the economics of content creation. Join us as we sit down with Prateek Thakker, one of Bangalore’s pioneer food and lifestyle creators who successfully built a powerhouse brand from scratch, commanding a community of over 100k followers on Instagram.
Forget the surface-level glamour—we are getting into the real numbers. Prateek will share his journey of building a brand from the ground up, navigating the unspoken hurdles of the industry, and seizing the massive opportunities that open up once you crack the algorithm.
Most importantly, we are breaking down the money math. From monetization structures and brand deal dynamics to treating influence as genuine equity, this session will dissect the true unit economics of the creator class. Whether you are an aspiring creator, an entreprenuer hoping to hire influencers to build your brand or just someone interested about a content creator's life, this is a blueprint you cannot afford to miss.
Networking opportunities will follow the event.

name: Feel. Experience. Design. - Design thinking workshop
description:
Design Thinking is a human-centred approach to problem-solving - one that starts not with solutions, but with genuine understanding of the people the problem affects. Originally developed in the world of product and service design, it is now used across industries, organisations, and social contexts to tackle everything from workplace culture to public policy.

The workshop takes participants through all five stages of Design Thinking - Empathise, Define, Ideate, Prototype, and Test - using the tools of Applied Theatre. Every stage is experienced in the body, not just understood in the mind.

Who is this for?
Professionals, practitioners, and curious thinkers who work with people, systems, or ideas - and want a deeper, more honest way of understanding the problems they solve. Useful for product managers, HR and L&D professionals, designers, consultants, educators, social sector workers, facilitators, and anyone who has sat through one too many sticky-note sessions and wanted something more real.

No prior knowledge of Design Thinking or theatre required. Curiosity is the only prerequisite.

About the Facilitator
Bejoy Balagopal - Co-founder of First Drop Theatre. Part engineer, part storyteller, long-time theatre practitioner. 25 years navigating corporate and creative worlds. Holds a Professional Certificate in Design Thinking and Innovation with AI from IIM Kozhikode.
Poster Description - Features a warm, textured background with soft circular shapes. The words - "Feel. Experience. Design." - are displayed in large, hand-lettered text, with a photograph of the facilitator in the upper right. A row of small sticky notes are scattered across the middle of the poster, each carrying a single word - Feel, Inhabit, Question, Build, Break, Discover.

name: Natural Dyeing Workshop for Beginners - Art Workshop
description:
Discover the fascinating world of natural colours in this hands-on Natural Dyeing Workshop designed for beginners. Learn how everyday natural materials such as onion peels, marigold flowers, sappan wood, indigo, and other plant-based ingredients can be transformed into beautiful, sustainable dyes for textiles.
The workshop will guide you through the complete dyeing process, starting with an introduction to natural dyes and the importance of fabric preparation. You will learn the fundamentals of mordanting : a crucial step that helps colours bond with the fabric and improves colour fastness. Through step-by-step demonstrations, participants will explore dye extraction, fabric preparation, dyeing techniques, and colour development using a range of natural ingredients.
The session will also introduce dye modification techniques, showing how simple modifiers can alter and expand the colour palette obtained from a single dye source. Participants will gain practical knowledge of sustainable textile practices while experimenting with colours and patterns.
As part of the workshop, you will create your own tie-dyed naturally dyed fabric samples and take them home, along with the confidence to continue exploring natural dyeing techniques on your own.
Duration: 2 hours Suitable for: Beginners, craft enthusiasts, students, designers, and anyone interested in sustainable textile practices.

name: Move through Math  - Book launch
description:
This event marks the release of Move Through Math, a handbook designed for parents, facilitators, and educators working with children aged 5–10. Rooted in the principles of embodied education, the book explores how abstract mathematical concepts can be understood through movement, sound, rhythm, and spatial awareness. By adapting movements from Bharatanatyam, the handbook offers a culturally grounded, multisensory approach to STEM learning.
Program Highlights:


Author Presentation: An overview of the educational framework and the philosophy of embodied pedagogy.


Live Demonstration: Selected mathematical concepts expressed through Bharatanatyam-inspired movement.


Interactive Segment: A guided, accessible movement exploration for the audience.


Q&A and Networking: Open discussion followed by a book signing.

name: Atta Galatta presents “Support Group” - Support Group
description:
People make up big cities, but what makes up people’s lives, hopefully not loneliness. Loneliness is an intensely painful state that arises out of a lack of intimacy and connectedness. People from every walk of life, even those with social contacts or pursuing successful careers, can experience sadness from the absence of belonging. This painful experience is felt in a myriad of contexts, such as breakups, disagreeing families, fear of rejection, unrelatability, shyness, and so on
This support group has been constituted to bring people struggling with disconnectedness in various contexts together for a shared experience of comfort, belonging, laughter, guidance, and above all, to break the cycle of sustained loneliness
The support is being organized and moderated by Nimesh George and Gokul R, who are practicing psychologists based in Bangalore

name: Dialogue with your Subconscious in the language of ART - No Small Talk. Real Inner Work
description:
Come join me as we set up a dialogue with our inner self. We'll curate a safe, judgment-free space to express & introspect, bypassing conscious wiring to access the subconscious mind. Art makes this possible.
Through this session, you’ll have unexpected insights and mini-epiphanies and leave with a conscious takeaway about your psyche. Bring nothing but intentional presence, because all materials will be provided.
Hosted by Aarushi Saxena:
I used art as a release for more than 20 years, but when I learned to read art and began using it as a means to interpret my intrapsychic landscape, that's when my healing journey really began. And it has been deeply rewarding! My aim is to be a communication facilitator for others to dialogue with their inner self and experience their own psyche breakthroughs. If you're looking for healing, this is an ideal pressure-free medium. Think of this session as a gentle preview of what can unfold over a longer timeline dedicated to spiritual art practices.
What We’ll Do:

Simple Breathwork & Energy Flow: Centering ourselves to prepare the mind.
Lots of ART: Engaging directly with the canvas to let the subconscious speak.
Some Introspection: Looking deeply into the visual language we create.

What You’ll Get:

Bold insights into your subconscious thought-loops, patterns, and limitations
An avenue for long-term spiritual art guidance
Included in your ticket is a private, 1-on-1 session to be set up within 8 weeks post-workshop.

name: Plaeto Shoe Painting Workshop - Painting workshop by Plaeto
description:
Plaeto's Sole Art Junior is a creative workshop designed for young artists and their parents to explore art together. Through hands-on activities, kids unleash their imagination and craft unique designs in a warm, playful setting. A celebration of creativity, self-expression, and the joy of making something truly one's own.
Age Group: 04-14 years

name: Fat2Fit – Intimate Reading Session - Book Reading
description:
Over five years ago, I stood in front of a mirror at 110 kilos and made a promise to myself. That promise changed my life.
Fat2Fit is not a book about weight loss. It is a story about resilience, self-respect, healing, second chances, and discovering what becomes possible when we refuse to give up on ourselves.
I would love for you to join me for an intimate reading and conversation as I share stories from the book, the lessons behind the journey, and reflections that never made it onto social media.

name: Ghost of Garrincha - Paperclip Live - Storytelling
description:
The Paperclip is an independent media platform widely followed on social media for its deeply researched, authentic storytelling in an era of viral misinformation. Known for turning complex histories into compelling narratives, it now brings its most powerful football stories to a live audience for the first time in the city.
With the FIFA World Cup going on, this show taps into a moment when football is already on everyone’s mind - but goes far beyond the game. It speaks to an urban audience that is globally aware, engaged with geopolitics, and curious about the world beyond headlines. Blending football with power, politics, and human stories, the experience is designed to be highly engaging - even for those who don’t follow the sport. It’s not just a show for fans, but for anyone who enjoys sharp, thought-provoking storytelling.

name: Abstract Art Workshop  - Art Workshop
description:
Hi, I'm Thomas, an artist, content creator, and graphic designer. I'm hosting an art workshop at Atta Galatta on the 11th of July. The workshop will be a 2 hour secession, that's going to be similar to a crash course on the basic as well as the advanced techniques of abstract art.
 
In the workshop, I will teach you some of the important avenues that would help you monetize your artwork as well. You will also understand just how therapeutic and calming this art form really is.
 
Key pointers on pricing, how you could turn your art into merchandise, and how to go about the framing and sales aspects will also be covered during the course of the 2 hours.

name: Get in touch with your Body - Body Awareness Workshop
description:
“When was the last time you played without worrying about being good at it?”
As children, play came naturally. We explored, experimented, imagined, and expressed ourselves freely. Somewhere along the way, many of us traded play for productivity, performance, and perfection.
Get In Touch With Your Body is a playful movement and expressive arts workshop that invites you to return to that space of curiosity. Through creative movement, simple art-based activities, and shared experiences, you'll explore what happens when you let go of "doing it right" and allow yourself to simply engage, create, and move.
Play is more than fun. It helps us become more flexible, creative, connected, and present. It opens the door to self-expression, reduces stress, nurtures resilience, and reminds us that growth doesn't always have to be serious.
No dance experience. No art experience. No pressure to perform.
Just bring your curiosity, your willingness to explore, and a little room for play.
Come move, create, connect, and rediscover the joy of being fully alive in your body.

name: Auto Akka and the Hot Seat - Book reading and workshop
description:
Bangalore is sizzling! And young Ravi’s favourite blue, sticker-covered water bottle is officially missing. Jump into the hot seat with Auto Akka, the city's coolest auto rickshaw driver, for an interactive story reading and creative workshop! Based on the children's storybook Auto Akka and the Hot Seat, this fun reading session will take us all on a fast-paced hunt through bustling streets while exploring the real-world realities of urban heat and climate resilience. After the book reading, children will get hands-on and creative, sketching, designing, and inventing their very own climate resilient, cool autos to beat the heat!
About the Facilitators: 
Ujjvala Krishna is a climate and urban researcher drawn to the intersections of people, policy, and the built environment. Believing that cities are simultaneously wonderful and disastrously ad-hoc, her work explores the friction and negotiations between the various systems that shape our urban spaces. As an architect by training, she is perpetually asking, "how did this really get built?" - seeking to bridge the gap between the chaotic, lived reality of the city, and what shapes it.
Radha Patkar is a visual communication designer from Mumbai, currently based in Bangalore. She works across illustration, comics, and visual storytelling, creating stories inspired by everyday life. Her work draws from the people, places, and small moments that often go unnoticed, turning them into visual narratives that feel familiar, observant, and human.
Nithya Gowda is an urban planner and community engagement specialist. She has worked at the intersection of mobility, climate resilience, urban ecology, and grassroots engagement. Her work consistently turns community knowledge into planning evidence: from leading walkability audits across 300 km of Bengaluru's footpaths to co-authoring a nationally replicable safe routes to school guidebook. Fluent in Kannada, she engages directly with communities that planning processes most often leave out.

name: Unhurried Afternoon - Sip and Paint Experience
description: An Unhurried Afternoon is a beginner-friendly sip-and-paint experience designed for those looking to slow down and spend a creative afternoon away from the rush of everyday life. Over two relaxed hours, participants will create a cozy café-inspired acrylic painting featuring a sunflower and blue coffee mug while enjoying a beverage and good company. No prior painting experience is required. All materials are provided, and participants will be guided step-by-step through the process. Ticket includes painting materials and one beverage

name: Taishogoto to Bulbul Tarang - Informative Session and Musical Perfomance
description: An informative session for young minds on the Genesis and Evolution of the Bulbul Tarang, featuring a live demonstration and exhibition and a concert.  Experience the sounds, stories, and evolution of Bulbul Tarang  bridging the past and present.  Join us for an unforgettable evening celebrating the timeless charm and global journey of this remarkable instrument.

name: Magnet and Keychain Making Workshop - Art Workshop
description:
Join me for a creative workshop of magnet and keychain making using acrylic paints.
This is a beginner-friendly session where you can explore your artistic talent and express them in the form of unique and vibrant keychains and magnets that capture your vivid imagination. Personalised art pieces to take home that would be an expression of your inner self!
What’s provided
All the materials will be provided, along with expert guidance
What you need to bring
Just bring your creative vibes along
